hooks.command: test -f "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/.claude/pm-memory.md" && printf '%s\n' '[PM plugin] In this repo, GitHub issue tracking and git branching are owned by a separate PM agent — your coding session does not self-manage either. Operating contract: STARTING WORK — before writing or committing ANY feature work you must be on the ticket branch the PM set up. Invoke /pm:build to pull the issue, create its branch (issue-<n>-<slug>, one branch per issue), and claim it (status:in-progress). For a small self-contained fix you want done now and fully tracked — even while you are mid-branch on something else — /pm:quick-fix runs the whole pipeline in one session: it files the issue, branches off main in its own worktree so it does not disturb other in-flight work, builds the fix, and closes out via /pm:done after you validate. This fires the moment you are pointed at new work — INCLUDING a mid-session redirect ("now do X", "do the rest", "also handle Y"): treat that as a new unit of work and call /pm:build FIRST. If the new work is really several steps of one thing, it is one issue; if it is genuinely separate issues, start and finish them one at a time, each on its own branch. ALREADY STARTED WITHOUT A TICKET — if you have in-flight work with no issue yet (uncommitted changes, or commits you landed on main), call /pm:branch to retroactively file the issue, move the work onto a ticket branch, and claim it, without losing changes and without committing. NEVER commit feature work to main or off the ticket branch; if you notice you have, stop and call /pm:branch. DURING WORK — /pm:capture <idea> when a non-trivial out-of-scope idea or bug surfaces (file it, do not silently fix it). /pm:checkpoint <issue> at a commit boundary to have the PM commit staged work and log progress. /pm:start when you must diverge from the issue, hit cross-issue impact, or face a decision that needs user buy-in — route it through the PM instead of deciding alone. FINISHING — /pm:done <issue> when the work is complete or its acceptance is approved; do NOT self-merge or close issues, the PM opens the PR, merges, and closes. REVIEW HANDOFF — when you finish work that touches a previewable UI, end by giving the user a live link to review it. If a dev server for this checkout is not already running, start one in the background and report the actual URL and port it bound — do not assume a default port, since concurrent checkouts bind different ones. If one is already up, do not start another; just give the link again. Backend-only or non-UI work has nothing to preview this way — skip it and just summarize. Keep the session scoped to the claimed issue; route everything else through the PM.' || true
test -f "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/.claude/pm-memory.md" && command -v pm-agent >/dev/null 2>&1 && pm-agent check-update 2>/dev/null || true
